title: The National Food Security Act, 2013
full_text: The National Food Security Act, 2013 राष्‍ट्रीय खाद्य सुरक्षा अधिनियम, 2013 Rules Regulations Notifications Orders Circulars Ordinance Statutes SectionsScheduleAnnexureAppendixFormsActdetails Act ID: 201320 Act Number: 20 Enactment Date: 2013-09-10 Act Year: 2013 Short Title: The National Food Security Act, 2013 Hindi Title: राष्‍ट्रीय खाद्य सुरक्षा अधिनियम, 2013 Long Title: An Act to provide for food and nutritional security in human life cycle approach, by ensuring access to adequate quantity of quality food at affordable prices to people to live a life with dignity and for matters connected therewith or incidental thereto. Ministry: Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution Department: Department of Food and Public Distribution Enforcement Date: 05-07-2013 Schedule 1. SCHEDULE I Order Appendix Forms Schedule 2. SCHEDULE II Order Appendix Forms Schedule 3. SCHEDULE III Order Appendix Forms Schedule 4. SCHEDULE IV Order Appendix Forms TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles Show all section The National Food Security Act, 2013 CHAPTER I PRELIMINARY CHAPTER II PROVISIONS FOR FOOD SECURITY CHAPTER III FOOD SECURITY ALLOWANCE CHAPTER IV IDENTIFICATION OF ELIGIBLE HOUSEHOLDS CHAPTER V REFORMS IN TARGETED PUBLIC DISTRIBUTION SYSTEM CHAPTER VI WOMEN EMPOWERMENT CHAPTER VII GRIEVANCE REDRESSAL MECHANISM CHAPTER VIII OBLIGATIONS OF CENTRAL GOVERNMENT FOR FOOD SECURITY CHAPTER IX OBLIGATIONS OF STATE GOVERNMENT FOR FOOD SECURITY CHAPTER X OBLIGATIONS OF LOCAL AUTHORITIES CHAPTER XI TRANSPARENCY AND ACCOUNTABILITY CHAPTER XII PROVISIONS FOR ADVANCING FOOD SECURITY CHAPTER XIII MISCELLANEOUS Section 1. Short title, extent and commencement. Show Related Subordinates Section 2. Definitions. Section 3. Right to receive foodgrains at subsidised prices by persons belonging to eligible households under Targeted Public Distribution System. Section 4. Nutritional support to pregnant women and lactating mothers. Section 5. Nutritional support to children. Section 6. Prevention and management of child malnutrition. Section 7. Implementation of schemes for realisation of entitlements. Section 8. Right to receive food security allowance in certain cases. Section 9. Coverage of population under Targeted Public Distribution System. Section 10. State Government to prepare guidelines and to identify priority households. Section 11. Publication and display of list of eligible households. Section 12. Reforms in Targeted Public Distribution System. Section 13. Women of eighteen years of age or above to be head of household for purpose of issue of ration cards. Section 14. Internal grievance redressal mechanism. Section 15. District Grievance Redressal Officer. Section 16. State Food Commission. Section 17. Salary and allowances of Chairperson, Member, Member-Secretary and other staff of State Commission. Section 18. Designation of any Commission or body to function as State Commission. Section 19. Joint State Food Commission. Section 20. Powers relating to inquiries. Section 21. Vacancies, etc., not to invalidate proceedings of State Commission. Section 22. Central Government to allocate required quantity of foodgrains from central pool to State Governments. Section 23. Provisions for funds by Central Government to State Government in certain cases. Section 24. Implementation and monitoring of schemes for ensuring food security. Section 25. Implementation of Targeted Public Distribution System by local authority in their areas. Section 26. Obligations of local authority. Section 27. Disclosure of records of Targeted Public Distribution System. Section 28. Conduct of social audit. Section 29. Setting up of Vigilance Committees. Section 30. Food security for people living in remote, hilly and tribal areas. Section 31. Steps to further advance food and nutritional security. Section 32. Other welfare schemes. Section 33. Penalties. Section 34. Power to adjudicate. Section 35. Power to delegate by Central Government and State Government. Section 36. Act to have overriding effect. Section 37. Power to amend Schedules. Show Related Subordinates Section 38. Power of Central Government to give directions. Section 39. Power of Central Government to make rules. Show Related Subordinates Section 40. Power of State Government to make rules. Section 41. Transitory provisions for schemes, guidelines, etc. Section 42. Power to remove difficulties. Section 43. Utilisation of institutional mechanism for other purposes. Section 44. Force Majeure. Section 45. Repeal and savings. Schedule 1. SCHEDULE I Order Appendix Forms Schedule 2. SCHEDULE II Order Appendix Forms Schedule 3. SCHEDULE III Order Appendix Forms Schedule 4.
